The '''GE3-series protocol droid''' was a [[Limb/Legends|bipedal]] [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] manufactured by [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]]. Vaguely resembling later models from [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]], GE3s were one of the most common and best-selling [[Droid/Legends|droids]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during both the [[Jedi Civil War]] and [[Dark Wars]].<ref name="KOTORCG" />

==Characteristics==
They could be programmed for multiple functions, including waiter, bartender, tour guide, receptionist, secretary, errand-runner, diplomatic aide, [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]], or even [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. Their programming was specifically designed for social tasks, with an emphasis on Human-cyborg relations and a massive database of languages, cultures, and customs of thousands of species. They were equipped with [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulators]] with versatile translator units.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

Demand for the droids was high, and they were a common sight on nearly every planet in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka]] also sold GE3-series droids in bulk at a significant discount to planetary governments.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> While searching the [[Jedi Enclave]]'s sublevel, the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]] found a note about a series of Czerka-produced droids left by the Enclave's head technician, [[Dergar Chester]].

There were numerous models, physically identical, but differing in internal details and skills. Each GE3 had a full designation which included the suffix "GE3" at the end, for example, [[S-0D3-GE3]].

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[canon]] short comic "[[Off the Rails (comic story)|Off the Rails]]," the [[Unidentified dealer droid|featured croupier droid]] greatly resembles<ref name="Off the Rails">{{RebelsMagCite|31|Off the Rails (comic story)}}</ref> the GE3-series protocol droid.<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>
